articles.description:
Firehouse Subs brings back the Thanksgiving Turkey Sub for a limited time this fall. The seasonal sandwich is joined by new sides and a dessert, including Gravy, Extra Cheesy Mac & Cheese, and a Double Chocolate Chip Cookie, to cover the general aspects of a Thanksgiving meal (i.e., turkey, sides, and dessert).

- Thanksgiving Turkey Sub -  Carved turkey breast, savory stuffing, sweet cranberry sauce, and mayo, served hot on a toasted sub roll.
- New Gravy - A "rich, savory gravy with notes of garlic, roasted turkey, and vegetable stock." Firehouse Subs recommends dunking your sandwich in it.
- New Extra Cheesy Mac & Cheese - Cavatappi noodles with a creamy mix of American, cheddar, Neufchatel, Monterey Jack, Queso, and asadero cheeses.
- New Double Chocolate Chip Cookie - Made with a cocoa-based dough packed with semi-sweet chocolate and creamy white chocolate chunks.
The sub and new menu items are available now at participating restaurants nationwide.
Photo via Firehouse Subs.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Firehouse Subs Brings Back Thanksgiving Turkey Sub

articles.description:
Wendy's launches new Chicken Tenders (or "Tendys" as they're colloquially calling them, as the term happens to rhyme with the chain's name) as a new core menu item. The tenders are joined by six new dipping sauces.

The new chicken strips are described as "flavor-packed and savory, made with seasoned, crispy breading and juicy, all-white-meat chicken." The new dipping sauces include:


Wendy's Signature - A "creamy sauce with hints of black pepper, hot sauce, and tons of savory goodness."
Sweet Chili - A "Sweet and subtly spicy sauce with a touch of tang, including notes of sriracha, garlic, and ginger."
Scorchin' Hot - A "creamy hot sauce with a heat that builds."
Creamy Ranch - "Extra creamy and extra rich."
Honey BBQ - "A little bit sweet, a little bit smoky."
Honey Mustard - "Sweet honey and lightly tangy mustard with a rich Dijon flavor."


Wendy's Chicken Tenders are available now at locations nationwide. They can be purchased in three- or four-piece orders, which include two dipping sauces. Combo meals and a Kid's Meal (with two pieces and a dipping sauce) are also available.

articles.description:
El Pollo Loco launches new Double Chicken Burrito Bowls in two varieties for a limited time this fall.

There's the Queso Crunch Double Chicken Burrito Bowl and the Street Corn Double Chicken Burrito Bowl.
The Queso Crunch Double Chicken Burrito Bowl (pictured) features pinto beans and seasoned rice, topped with warm queso blanco, a double portion of citrus-marinated flame-grilled chicken, shredded jack cheese, handmade guacamole, chopped cilantro, and salsa fresca. Tortilla strips are included on the side.
The Street Corn Double Chicken Burrito Bowl is inspired by elotes and consists of seasoned rice and pinto beans, topped with a double portion of citrus-marinated flame-grilled chicken, creamy chipotle sauce, handmade guacamole, shredded jack cheese, crumbled cotija, roasted corn, roasted red bell peppers, chopped cilantro, and salsa fresca.
Prices vary, but my local El Pollo Loco in Los Angeles charges $12.79 for each bowl.
El Pollo Loco is also offering the Fam Feast as a limited-time, discounted group meal exclusively to its rewards members. The Fam Feast includes eight pieces of citrus-marinated flame-grilled chicken, three large sides, eight warm flour or corn tortillas (or a large order of tortilla chips), and four churros. Prices vary, but it costs $29.99 near me with legs and thighs ($34.49 for mixed chicken pieces).
Photo via El Pollo Loco.

articles.description:
Little Caesars' Ultimate Cheesesteak Pizza features a large round original crust topped with a cheesy white sauce, mozzarella, seasoned sliced steak, onions, green peppers, and mushrooms.

I bought one for $16.99. The price is more than any listed pizza on the menu (the next closest in price is the 5 Meat Feast for $14.99).



The pizza hit the cheesesteak theme very well. Between the mozzarella and the cheesy white sauce, it was very cheesy with a nice stretch and a surprising buttery note.



The steak wasn't abundant or spread evenly, but it offered a tender bite and good, lightly seasoned meatiness.



The veggies were also good. They were just cooked through, so they weren't mushy and still had a decent bite. Additionally, they had a strong but not overpowering presence taste-wise, particularly the mushrooms. Just a note: Little Caesars in California uses fresh mushrooms, given the proximity to all the farms in Central California. That is not necessarily the case in other states, so your mileage may vary.
The crust was okay. It was sturdy with some chew to it and some crisping here and there.



Overall, Little Caesars' Ultimate Cheesesteak Pizza hit the nail on the head as far as cheesesteak pizzas go. The flavors and textures hit the right notes. However, while the cheesiness was great, I would have liked at more beef (I get that steak is expensive, but wonder if ground beef would have worked). Also, the price is a bit steep for what I got.
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Little Caesars - Ultimate Cheesesteak Pizza

articles.description:
Taco Bell's Slow-Roasted Chicken Nachos Grande features a double portion of slow-roasted chicken (compared to the Cantina Chicken Soft Taco), refried beans, warm nacho cheese sauce, a three-cheese blend (mozzarella, pepper jack, and cheddar), pico de gallo, reduced-fat sour cream, and guacamole, served over a bed of tortilla chips.

It came in a box and cost me $6.99.
The box was filled pretty well with a decent amount of chicken, toppings, and chips. Topping coverage was pretty good, although I always find myself wishing for just a little more nacho cheese sauce.



As with a lot of things at Taco Bell, the combination of flavors was very familiar, as the Grande Nachos aren't much different from Taco Bell's previous nachos in a box (or regular menu Nachos BellGrande, for that matter). Compared to Nachos BellGrande, you get more food for a similar price ($5.99 for the seasoned beef version of both at my local Taco Bell). I would guess it to be a third more food. It's definitely shareable.
What stood out from the Grande Nachos over  Nachos BellGrande was the guacamole and pico de gallo, which added an acidity that the latter lacks (it has sour cream and tomatoes, but they don't add much).
The chicken was also interesting, as Taco Bell's limited-time nachos usually come with beef (either seasoned ground beef or steak). The slow-roasted chicken was tender and meaty with a nice seasoning to it. I much prefer it to the chain's previous chicken, which was milder in flavor and less satisfying texture-wise.



Overall, Taco Bell's Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos get a thumbs up from me. While familiar, there was a nice variety of flavors and textures to go with a good amount of food for the money. 
Nutritional Info - Taco Bell - Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos
Calories - 1100
Fat - 59g (Saturated Fat - 9g)
Sodium - 1720mg
Carbs - 111g (Sugar - 6g)
Protein - 32g
Read more at Brand Eating!
articles.title: Review: Taco Bell - Slow-Roasted Chicken Grande Nachos

Jalapeno Cheese Curds (pictured) will arrive on October 15, 2025 (apparently, the 10th anniversary of National Cheese Curd Day) and will be available for a limited time through October 31, 2025, while supplies last. They're a spicy take on Culver's classic Wisconsin Cheese Curds made with bits of jalapeno pepper and cheddar cheese.
The CurderBurger features a Deluxe ButterBurger topped with a breaded and fried patty of cheese (a blend of yellow and white cheddar). It arrives on September 29, 2025, and will be available through October 14, 2025, while supplies last. It first started as an April Fools' joke in 2021, but then they made it into an actual burger, and this is the fifth time they've brought it back.
In addition to the limited-time menu items, Culver's first-ever Curdtoberfest will include themed decor at its restaurants and an online sweepstakes with "free Wisconsin Cheese Curds for a year" and other prizes.
